LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BUFFALO PUMPS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. FAILURE TO SUSTAIN BURDEN OF PROOF ON THE ISSUE OF ORIGINAL OR REPLACEMENT PARTS. SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 6 B AND 6 C FAIL TO REQUIRE PLAINTIFF TO DISTINGUISH BETWEEN THE TWO. IF ADMIRAL HORNE'S DECLARATION HAD BEEN PRESENTED IN THE MOVING PAPERS, THE COURT COULD CONSIDER IT; IT CANNOT DO SO WHEN THE DECLARATION IS FILED WITH THE REPLY. THE COURT DECLINES TO ADOPT THE DUTY TO WARN THEORY AS TO REPLACEMENT PARTS. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-16-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FLOWSERVE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
Anthony Chiosso of Jackson & Wallace (982-6300) for defendant and moving party

No other appearances. The matter was not argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion for summary judgment is denied; failure to sustain burden of proof. Defendant has not demonstrated that plaintiff is unable to prove exposure to defendant's product with asbestos. Motion for summary adjudication is granted as to causes of action for false representation, intentional tort and punitive damages. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-16-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BW/IP, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
Anthony Chiosso of Jackson & Wallace (982-6300) for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment is denied; failure to sustain burden of proof. Decedent identified defendant's pumps; there is evidence that defendant's pumps contained asbestos and defendant has failed to demonstrate that the pumps decedent worked on did not contain asbestos. Motion for summary adjudication is granted as to punitive damages. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/xAH) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BUFFALO PUMPS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
James O'Brien of Jackson & Wallace, LLP (982-6300) for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Failure to sustain burden of proof on the issue of original or replacement parts. Special interrogatories 6 b and 6 c fail to require plaintiff to distinguish between the two. If Admiral Horne's declaration had been presented in the moving papers, the Court could consider it; it cannot do so when the declaration is filed with the reply. The Court declines to adopt the duty to warn theory as to replacement parts. Summary adjudication is granted as to the 3rd and 4th causes of action and punitive damages. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
